trade.summary: Crude oil and LNG dominate bilateral trade, with the UAE as one of Japan's top energy suppliers. Japan exports automobiles, machinery, electronics, and steel. Japan imports approximately 25% of its crude oil from the UAE. The relationship is anchored by energy security on both sides.

diplomatic.summary: Japan and the UAE maintain a strong strategic partnership grounded primarily in energy security. Japan values Gulf stability highly as an energy-dependent economy. High-level exchanges are frequent. Japan invested in UAE's Ruwais LNG project. Japanese PM has visited UAE regularly.

military.summary: Japan's constitutional constraints limit military cooperation. Japan participates in Gulf anti-piracy operations and has engaged in Gulf security dialogues. Japan deployed a destroyer and maritime patrol aircraft to the Gulf of Oman for information gathering. No arms transfers.

financial.summary: Japanese trading companies (Mitsui, JERA, INPEX) have equity stakes in UAE upstream oil and LNG projects. ADNOC has sold stakes in its pipelines to Japanese investors. UAE sovereign funds have invested in Japanese equities and infrastructure. Japan Bank for International Cooperation has funded UAE projects.

energy.summary: Energy is the cornerstone of the UAE-Japan relationship. Japan imports approximately 25% of its crude oil from the UAE annually (~1 million barrels/day). INPEX holds a 10% stake in ADNOC's concessions. JERA signed a major LNG deal with ADNOC. Japan is the UAE's largest single energy customer.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration in clean energy (hydrogen, ammonia), robotics, and space. Japan and UAE collaborate on the UAE Mars Hope Probe follow-on missions. JAXA and UAE Space Agency have MOUs. Japanese technology companies are active in UAE smart city projects.
